title: triteleia crocea
intro: Triteleia crocea, also known as the Golden Dandelion or Western Bells, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Asphodelaceae family. It is native to the western United States and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. Triteleia crocea can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
